recent_calls.summary: The user, Calia from American Home Shield, called to inquire about the out-of-pocket cost for a customer related to a specific dispatch number. The agent informed her that exact pricing could not be provided during the call but assured that a licensed technician or billing specialist would follow up. The user declined to provide a phone number for follow-up and ended the call shortly after.

The user called to speak with Daniel Lagars regarding a drain pan issue on their year-old HVAC system that is not covered under warranty. They sought clarification on why the warranty does not cover it, suspecting faulty installation or a bad part. The agent confirmed the details and arranged for Daniel to return the call at the user's preferred times.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully within a short call duration.

The user called to inquire about inviting the company to apply for Better Business Bureau accreditation and was advised to send an email with the details. The user declined to provide contact information and ended the call shortly after.

The user called to confirm an appointment for April 29th and inquired about cancellation notifications. The agent explained the process and requested a home warranty dispatch number, but the user declined to provide it and expressed frustration about repeating information to multiple people. The call ended without the user providing further details or requesting additional services.
The user requested to speak with a human agent, and the initial agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully within a short call duration.

The user called to request a quote for an air conditioner upgrade, specifically for a full unit replacement and total cost, with a preference to receive the quote before the end of the day. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would reach out soon. The user also expressed interest in home maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call experience.
The user called to inquire about a dispatch service and was asked by the agent to specify the type of service needed. The call ended shortly after without further details or resolution.

The user called to inquire about a bid related to their home warranty system for a cooling service, but the agent did not complete the conversation before the user hung up. The agent attempted to gather more details about the cooling issue.
The user called to schedule an appointment related to home warranty service. The agent began gathering details about the type of service needed but the call ended abruptly before completion.

The user, Linda Martin, called to schedule installation for a newly purchased toilet. The agent collected correct contact details and service address, confirmed the preferred afternoon time, and provided available slots. The call ended with the user requesting to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred.

Linda Mays called to schedule a service appointment for her air conditioner, which is not cooling properly. The agent confirmed her details and booked a provisional appointment for April 22, 2026, at 12 PM. The call ended with the agent transferring the call to a live agent due to an address discrepancy raised by the user.

James called to schedule a service appointment for his air conditioning unit, which is cooling but has a non-working fan suspected to be a capacitor issue. The agent confirmed the details and informed James that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will contact him soon. James declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Rebecca from American Home Shield called to report that a member's unit was not working and had worsened after a completed service. The agent collected the dispatch number, member details, service address, and arranged for a follow-up visit as soon as possible. The call ended with confirmation of the details and no further requests from the user.

The user called to check if their service appointment for their AC and furnace had been rescheduled. The agent confirmed the details and offered available time slots, but the user requested to speak with a live person, leading to a successful call transfer to a live agent.

The user, Beverly Haynes, called to respond to a message about servicing her air conditioner, which is not pulling air. She requested a service appointment for Friday at 10 AM, confirmed her details, and the agent assured her that the team would follow up to confirm the appointment. The call ended with the user satisfied and the appointment request successfully recorded.

The user called to inquire about scheduling or confirming an appointment for AC service. The agent engaged the user to gather more details about the air conditioning issue. The call ended shortly after without confirming an appointment.

Karen Bernstein called Budget to inquire if they replaced her water heater about three years ago at her condo. The agent confirmed the address and assured Karen that the team would check the records and follow up with her. The call ended with the agent confirming the next steps and offering further assistance if needed.

Bernard called to schedule a routine air conditioner checkup before turning on the system for the season. The agent booked an appointment for April 21st at 4 PM and provided the main contact number. The call ended with the agent transferring Bernard to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed a successful call transfer.
The user called and was greeted by the agent Sophie, who then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 19 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called to speak with Daniel, who was unavailable. The agent offered to pass along a message and inform Daniel to call the user back.

The user called to reschedule a heating and cooling appointment and requested to speak with a live person. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The user called customer service and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, facilitated the transfer without any issues.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and the user responding with frustration. The user hung up shortly after without further interaction.

The user called to report an invalid callback number they received from the heating and cooling department. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Diane Fuchs, called to request a service appointment for a non-working upstairs thermostat at her home. She initially requested a morning appointment on May 7th, but no slots were available, so she tried for May 8th morning and then afternoon, but no slots were available for those times either. The call ended without scheduling an appointment.

The user inquired about how to pay the amount due on their credit card and when they would receive the payment details. The agent explained the process of receiving a payment link via email after the quote is ready and offered to connect the user to a billing representative for further assistance. The call ended with the agent transferring the user to the billing team.

The user, Wendy Cunningham, called to schedule a service appointment for her air conditioning system, which is intermittently not working. The agent successfully booked an appointment for Friday at 12 PM and provided information about the dispatch fee. The user declined to provide feedback but was satisfied with the service.

Pam from Merric Home Shields called to inquire about the completion status of a cooling job with dispatch number 31678889. The agent requested more details and a contact number for follow-up. The call ended after the agent asked for the best phone number to reach Pam.
The user called after receiving a missed call from the agent's number. The agent acknowledged this and promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called looking for Nicholas Marcheno, stating it was his dental office, but the agent explained that this was Budget's service line and offered assistance with Budget services. The user declined and ended the call without requesting further help.
The agent initiated the call by asking the user for their service address ZIP code, but the user hung up shortly after without responding.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user, Michelle Weisberg, called to report a slow-draining tub and requested a service appointment. The agent provided available time slots, and the user provisionally booked an appointment for Tuesday, April 28th at 10 AM. The call ended with the user indicating they would call back.

The user called to address an issue with their contract where their name was incorrectly spelled. The agent assured the user that the name would be corrected and the contract resent so the installation of heating and air conditioning could be scheduled. The call ended with the user planning to call back after signing the corrected contract.

The user called to schedule an urgent service appointment for an AC unit leak at their home in Pacific, Missouri. The agent provided the earliest available slots for the appointment and agreed to flag the request as urgent for the team to review for earlier openings. The user confirmed the urgency and preferred the Tuesday appointment.
The user returned a call from the number and spoke briefly with the agent, who then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The user called and requested to speak with Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user to the appropriate live agent and successfully transferred the call.
The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly without any interaction or resolution. The agent attempted to engage the user, but there was no response before the user hung up.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, facilitated the transfer without any issues.

Krista Rhodes called to inquire about Vernon's arrival time, but the agent could not find an appointment in the system. The user requested to be transferred to a live representative, and the call was successfully transferred.

Jim called to schedule a routine spring air conditioning inspection as part of his paid maintenance plan. The agent confirmed the appointment details, including the service address and that any day works for the appointment, and verified the callback number. The call concluded with the user declining to provide feedback.

The user, Milton Guzman, a property manager, called to schedule a service appointment for a hot water tank that is not lighting. The agent confirmed the details, including the service address and contact information, and scheduled the earliest available appointment for the week. The user provided positive feedback about the quickness and effectiveness of the service.
The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly after a brief greeting from the agent without any meaningful interaction or assistance provided.

The user received a call from Budget's after-hours assistant, Evie, who clarified that she does not make outgoing calls and offered assistance. The user indicated they did not need any help and ended the call promptly.
No conversation happened.
